title: Linux Command Line Mastery
subtitle: From first cd to scripting servers — the shell skills every engineer eventually needs
description: A practical course on the Linux command line as it's used in 2026. Covers the filesystem, processes, text manipulation, shell scripting, package management, networking, and the production patterns developers actually use day-to-day. Optimized for engineers who write code but want fluency in the terminal that runs their systems.
audience: Developers, data scientists, sysadmins who want to work confidently on Linux systems. Some prior exposure to a terminal helps; not required.
prerequisites: Have access to a Linux machine, WSL, or Mac terminal
outcomes: Navigate the filesystem fluently — files, directories, permissions
Manipulate text with grep, sed, awk, jq
Write reliable shell scripts that handle errors
Use SSH for remote work and key management
Compose Unix tools with pipes for real workflows

modules.title: Filesystem and core commands
modules.objective: Move around, find things, manage files confidently.

lessons.title: The shell, your home, and ls/cd/pwd
Files: cp, mv, rm, ln, touch
Permissions: chmod, chown, the octal model

modules.title: Text manipulation
modules.objective: The Unix tools that turn lines into answers.

modules.title: Processes, jobs, and system view
modules.objective: See what's running and control it.

lessons.title: ps, top, htop — knowing your processes
systemd basics — services that survive reboot
Error handling: set -euo pipefail and traps
Practical scripts: parse args, read files, exit codes
curl, wget, and HTTP in the shell
Package managers: apt, dnf, brew, pacman
